observations.summary: Some people respond to grief by discarding all remnants of what they loved, starting anew with nothing.

evidence.quote: Some people throw everything away when they lose the thing they love most—burn it all, scatter the ashes, start fresh with empty hands.

observations.notes: This claim highlights a motif of grief and loss responses.

observations.summary: Others respond to grief by clinging tightly to every fragment of the lost, to the point of suffocation.

evidence.quote: Others keep every thread, every cracked vessel, every scrap of parchment with nothing written on it, and pull it tighter and tighter until there is no room left to breathe.

observations.notes: This claim contrasts with the previous, emphasizing a different grief motif.

observations.summary: Aviel is characterized as someone who clings tightly to every fragment of what he has lost.
observations.notes: This grounds Aviel's grief style in the narrative.

observations.summary: Aviel's son is actively trying to undo his father's tight grip on grief, moving through darkness to do so.

evidence.quote: Which is relevant because it is exactly what his son was creeping through the dark to undo.

observations.notes: This sets up a narrative tension and motivation.

accepted_observations.summary: Aviel is characterized as someone who clings tightly to every fragment of what he has lost.
accepted_observations.notes: This grounds Aviel's grief style in the narrative.

accepted_observations.summary: Aviel's son is actively trying to undo his father's tight grip on grief, moving through darkness to do so.

evidence.quote: Which is relevant because it is exactly what his son was creeping through the dark to undo.

accepted_observations.notes: This sets up a narrative tension and motivation.
